MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH BRAVO APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JULIAN PARDO DE ZELA OF THE LAW FIRM OF ROPERS MAJESKI & BENTLEY, TEL. NO. (408)918-4545 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JMB CONSTRUCTION.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JMB CONSTRUCTION, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Denied. (CCP 437c.) Defendant has not met its initial burden of proof. Defendant moves for summary judgment, or alternatively summary adjudication, on the grounds that: (1) Plaintiff cannot prove an essential element of his two causes of action that Plaintiff had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition; and (2) Defendant's evidence demonstrates that it did not have actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition, negating an essential element of Plaintiff's claim. (Notice of Motion, p. 2:3-12.) Both causes of action are predicated on negligence. (Complaint, GN-1, Prem.L-2.) Actual or constructive knowledge is not an element of negligence or general premises liability. (CACI nos. 400, 1000.) Defendant has not met is initial burden to "conclusively negate a necessary element of the plaintiff's case or establish a complete defense and thereby demonstrate that under no hypothesis is there a material factual issue which requires the process of a trial." (Brooks v. Eugene Burger Management Corp. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 1611, 1618.) PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/EHG
=302/ehg
